blocks.body: Articles (articles) are small but very important words in the English language. A / An are indefinite articles for something general or new. The is a definite article for something specific or already known.

{ "title": "9. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "A - before a consonant SOUND: a book, a university.", "An - before a vowel SOUND: an apple, an hour.", "A/An - for something new, uncertain, professions.", "The — for something known, concrete, unique.", "Zero Ø — for general concepts, abstractions, plurals.", "Most countries, cities, names — without an article.", "Exceptions: the USA, the UK, the Netherlands (plural/union).", "The + adjective = a group of people: the rich, the poor." ] }
